name: mindmap_session_resume
description: Resume from last session — returns what the previous AI agent worked on, symbol-level diffs, project structure, and hot files. The single most important first call.

params.name: projectPath
params.description: Path to project root (auto-detected if omitted)

example:
mindmap_session_resume
→ Session #14 | Last: 2h ago
→ Changed: auth.ts (authenticate modified, refreshToken added)
→ Hot files: task.ts (12 edits), auth.ts (8 edits)

name: mindmap_session_start
description: Start tracking a new development session. Records a label and timestamp for change tracking and session summaries.

params.name: label
params.description: Human-readable session label, e.g. 'Add OAuth2 support'

example:
mindmap_session_start { label: 'Implement task filtering' }
→ Session #15 started at 14:32:01

name: mindmap_session_end
description: End the current session and persist a summary for future agents. Records what was accomplished, what changed, and any open items.

params.name: summary
params.description: Optional manual summary of what was accomplished

example:
mindmap_session_end { summary: 'Added task priority filtering and sort' }
→ Session #15 ended. 4 files changed, 3 functions added.

name: mindmap_session_summary
description: Get a summary of a past session by number or the current session. Shows label, duration, files changed, and functions added/modified.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: Session number to retrieve (latest if omitted)

example:
mindmap_session_summary { sessionId: 12 }
→ Session #12 'Fix auth bugs' | 45 min | 3 files | 2 fn modified

name: mindmap_session_diff
description: Get symbol-level diffs since the last session ended. Shows exactly which functions/classes were added, modified, or deleted.
example:
mindmap_session_diff
→ Added: refreshToken (auth.ts:52)
→ Modified: authenticate (auth.ts:12) — signature changed
→ Deleted: legacyLogin (auth.ts:80)

name: mindmap_set_project
description: Set or switch the active project directory for all subsequent Mind Map operations.
example:
mindmap_set_project { projectPath: '/home/user/my-app' }
→ Project set to my-app (TypeScript, 142 files)

name: mindmap_status
description: Check the health and status of the Mind Map system — index freshness, node/edge counts, memory usage, and last reindex time.
example:
mindmap_status
→ Index: fresh (2m ago) | 847 nodes, 2341 edges | Memory: 12MB

name: mindmap_reindex
description: Force a full re-index of the project. Use when results seem stale or after major refactors like branch switches.

params.name: force
params.description: Force full reindex even if cache is fresh

example:
mindmap_reindex { force: true }
→ Reindexed 142 files in 3.2s | 847 nodes, 2341 edges

name: mindmap_smart_search
description: Search for symbols by name with full context — returns signature, file, line number, callers, and callees in a single call. Preferred over basic search.

params.name: type
params.description: Filter by type: function, class, method, interface

example:
mindmap_smart_search { query: 'authenticate' }
→ AuthService.authenticate (auth.ts:12)
  Callers: LoginForm, AuthMiddleware
  Calls: validateToken, hashPassword, findByEmail

name: mindmap_search
description: Basic symbol search by name. Returns matching node IDs and basic info. Use mindmap_smart_search for richer results.
example:
mindmap_search { query: 'Task', type: 'interface' }
→ Task (types/task.ts:1), TaskFilter (types/task.ts:18)

name: mindmap_search_code
description: Grep-style text search across source code. Returns matching lines with context. Useful for finding string literals, comments, or patterns.

params.name: pattern
params.description: Text or regex pattern to search for

params.name: includes
params.description: Glob pattern for files to include, e.g. '*.ts'

name: mindmap_trace_dependencies
description: Trace all dependencies of a symbol — what it depends on and what depends on it. Shows the full dependency chain up to a configurable depth.

name: mindmap_find_references
description: Find all references to a symbol across the codebase. Shows every file and line where the symbol is used, imported, or referenced.

params.name: symbol
params.description: Symbol name to find references for

name: mindmap_semantic_search
description: Search by concept or description rather than exact name. Uses embeddings to find code related to a natural language query.

params.name: query
params.description: Natural language description, e.g. 'error handling logic'

example:
mindmap_semantic_search { query: 'authentication and login flow' }
→ authenticate (auth.ts:12) — 0.94 relevance
→ LoginForm (LoginForm.tsx:6) — 0.89 relevance
→ verifyJWT (auth.ts:12) — 0.85 relevance

name: mindmap_interaction_map
description: Get all routes, event handlers, API endpoints, or component interactions in the project. A high-level interaction overview.
example:
mindmap_interaction_map { type: 'routes' }
→ POST /api/tasks → TaskController.handleCreate
→ GET /api/tasks → TaskController.handleList
→ ...

name: mindmap_explain
description: Get everything about a symbol in one call — signature, parameters, return type, callers, callees, documentation, and file location. The most token-efficient way to understand code.
example:
mindmap_explain { symbol: 'authenticate' }
→ AuthService.authenticate(email, password): Promise<AuthToken>
→ File: src/services/auth.ts:12-32
→ Callers: LoginForm.onSubmit, AuthMiddleware.verifyJWT
→ Calls: validateToken, hashPassword, findByEmail
→ Doc: Authenticates user with email/password, returns JWT token

name: mindmap_get_code_snippet
description: Read the actual source code of a specific symbol — just the function/class body, not the whole file. Minimizes token usage when you need to see implementation.

params.name: symbol
params.description: Symbol name to get code for

name: mindmap_file_digest
description: Get a structured summary of a file without reading it — all exports, classes, functions, imports, and their relationships. Saves 3–10K tokens per file.
example:
mindmap_file_digest { file: 'src/services/auth.ts' }
→ AuthService (class, exported)
  ├─ authenticate (async method, 20 lines)
  ├─ validateToken (method, 18 lines)
  ├─ refreshToken (async method, 15 lines)
  └─ hashPassword (private async method, 5 lines)
→ Imports: Database, UserRepository, Logger

name: mindmap_file_skeleton
description: Get just the structural skeleton of a file — signatures only, no implementation. Even cheaper than file_digest for quick orientation.
example:
mindmap_file_skeleton { file: 'src/services/task.ts' }
→ class TaskService
    createTask(title, priority): Promise<Task>
    deleteTask(id): Promise<void>
    updateTask(id, data): Promise<Task>
    getTasks(filter?): Promise<Task[]>

name: mindmap_get_file_map
description: List all symbols defined in a file with their types, line numbers, and export status. Quick inventory of a file's contents.
example:
mindmap_get_file_map { file: 'src/db/database.ts' }
→ Database (class, L4, exported)
→ query (method, L18)
→ transaction (method, L35)
→ migrate (method, L58)

name: mindmap_get_signature
description: Get just the type signature of a symbol — the cheapest possible read. No callers, no body, just the declaration.
example:
mindmap_get_signature { symbol: 'createTask' }
→ async createTask(title: string, priority: Priority): Promise<Task>

name: mindmap_read_lines
description: Read specific line ranges from a file. More efficient than reading the whole file when you know exactly where to look.
example:
mindmap_read_lines { file: 'src/services/auth.ts', startLine: 12, endLine: 32 }
→ (returns 20 lines of the authenticate method)

name: mindmap_list_dir
description: List directory contents with file types, sizes, and symbol counts. Tree-view of project structure.
example:
mindmap_list_dir { path: 'src', depth: 1 }
→ src/
  ├─ services/ (3 files, 12 symbols)
  ├─ controllers/ (2 files, 8 symbols)
  ├─ components/ (4 files, 4 symbols)
  └─ App.tsx (1 symbol)

name: mindmap_digest
description: Full project summary in under 2000 tokens — tech stack, file count, key modules, architecture layers, and recent activity. The cheapest way to understand a project.
example:
mindmap_digest
→ TaskFlow (TypeScript + React + Express)
→ 142 files | 847 symbols | 4 layers
→ Key modules: auth, tasks, users, database
→ Last change: 2h ago (auth.ts)

name: mindmap_architecture
description: Get the architectural overview — layers, module boundaries, dependency direction, and coupling metrics. Understand how the project is organized.
example:
mindmap_architecture { format: 'text' }
→ Presentation: App, TaskList, TaskCard, LoginForm
→ Controllers: TaskController
→ Services: TaskService, AuthService
→ Data: UserRepository, Database

name: mindmap_project_map
description: Complete project map — every file, module, and their relationships. More detailed than digest, shows full dependency graph.
example:
mindmap_project_map
→ (full project tree with symbol counts and dependencies)

name: mindmap_layer_overview
description: See how code is organized into architectural layers (presentation, business, data, infrastructure) with metrics for each layer.
example:
mindmap_layer_overview
→ Presentation: 4 components (TaskList, TaskCard, LoginForm, App)
→ Business: 2 services, 1 controller (32 methods)
→ Data: 1 repository, 1 database class
→ Infrastructure: Logger, Config, Router

name: mindmap_changelog
description: Symbol-level changelog — which functions/classes were added, modified, or deleted since a given point. Much richer than git log.
example:
mindmap_changelog { since: 'last-session' }
→ ADDED: refreshToken (auth.ts:52)
→ MODIFIED: authenticate (auth.ts:12) — added rate limiting
→ DELETED: legacyLogin (auth.ts:80)

name: mindmap_verify_changes
description: Verify if cached/known code is still valid by checking content hashes. Avoids re-reading files that haven't changed.

params.name: symbols
params.description: List of symbol names to verify

name: mindmap_git_changes
description: Map git diffs to symbols — shows which functions/classes were affected by recent commits. Richer than raw git diff.

name: mindmap_hotspots
description: Find the most frequently modified files and functions. Identifies code that changes often and might need refactoring.
example:
mindmap_hotspots { limit: 5 }
→ 1. task.ts (12 edits) — createTask, updateTask
→ 2. auth.ts (8 edits) — authenticate
→ 3. TaskList.tsx (6 edits)

name: mindmap_what_changed
description: Quick overview of what changed recently — files modified, symbols affected, and whether the changes are breaking.

name: mindmap_file_history
description: Get the commit history for a specific file with symbol-level annotations showing what changed in each commit.
example:
mindmap_file_history { file: 'src/services/auth.ts', limit: 5 }
→ abc123 (2h ago): Modified authenticate — added rate limiting
→ def456 (1d ago): Added refreshToken method
→ ...

name: mindmap_remember
description: Store a fact or learning that persists across sessions. Future AI agents will have access to this knowledge without re-discovering it.
example:
mindmap_remember { fact: 'The auth service uses bcrypt with 12 rounds for password hashing', tags: ['auth', 'security'] }
→ Remembered (memory #42)

name: mindmap_recall
description: Recall previously stored facts and learnings. Search by keyword or tag to find relevant memories from past sessions.
example:
mindmap_recall { query: 'authentication' }
→ #42: The auth service uses bcrypt with 12 rounds
→ #38: JWT tokens expire after 1 hour, refresh tokens after 7 days
→ #35: Rate limiting is set to 5 attempts per minute

name: mindmap_decide
description: Record an architectural or design decision with rationale. Creates a persistent decision log that future agents can reference.

name: mindmap_get_decisions
description: View all recorded architectural decisions. Filter by topic or date range.

name: mindmap_compress
description: Compress verbose output (logs, errors, stack traces) into a concise summary. Reduces token usage for large outputs.
example:
mindmap_compress { content: '(500 lines of stack trace)', maxTokens: 200 }
→ TypeError in authenticate (auth.ts:25): Cannot read 'email' of undefined
  Called from: LoginForm.onSubmit → AuthService.authenticate
  Root cause: Missing null check on user lookup result

name: mindmap_impact_analysis
description: Analyze the blast radius of changing a symbol — what would break, what tests cover it, what downstream consumers are affected.
example:
mindmap_impact_analysis { symbol: 'Task', changeType: 'signature' }
→ HIGH IMPACT: 12 direct consumers, 23 transitive
→ Affected: createTask, updateTask, getTasks, TaskCard, TaskList
→ Tests: TaskService.test (3 tests), integration.test (2 tests)
→ Risk: MEDIUM — no external API consumers

name: mindmap_dead_code
description: Find unreachable or unused code — functions that are never called, exports that are never imported, dead branches.
example:
mindmap_dead_code
→ legacyLogin (auth.ts:80) — 0 callers
→ formatDate (utils.ts:12) — 0 callers
→ UNUSED EXPORT: DebugPanel (DebugPanel.tsx)

name: mindmap_classify_file
description: Classify which architectural layer a file belongs to — presentation, controller, service, repository, utility, or configuration.
example:
mindmap_classify_file { file: 'src/services/auth.ts' }
→ Layer: Business Logic (Service)
→ Depends on: Data layer (UserRepository, Database)
→ Consumed by: Controller layer (AuthMiddleware)

name: mindmap_health
description: Run health checks on the Mind Map system — index integrity, stale nodes, orphan edges, and performance metrics.
example:
mindmap_health
→ Index: HEALTHY
→ 847 nodes (0 stale), 2341 edges (0 orphan)
→ Avg query time: 12ms | Memory: 14MB
→ Last full reindex: 45m ago

name: mindmap_query_graph
description: Run a raw Cypher query against the knowledge graph for advanced analysis. Power-user tool for custom graph queries.

name: mindmap_trace_flow
description: Trace a feature flow end-to-end — from UI action through controllers, services, and data layer. Shows the complete execution path.
example:
mindmap_trace_flow { entry: 'handleCreate' }
→ Router.setupRoutes (POST /tasks)
  → AuthMiddleware.verifyJWT
    → AuthService.validateToken
  → TaskController.handleCreate
    → TaskService.createTask
      → Database.query (INSERT)
      → Logger.log

name: mindmap_debug_changes
description: When something broke — shows actual git diffs mapped to symbols, highlighting what changed and what might have caused the issue.

params.name: symptom
params.description: Description of the bug or error

example:
mindmap_debug_changes { symptom: 'Login returns 401' }
→ Recent changes to auth flow:
  authenticate (auth.ts:12): Added rate limiting check at line 18
  validateToken (auth.ts:34): Changed token expiry from 1h to 15m
→ Likely cause: Token expiry change (validateToken)

name: mindmap_file_before
description: Get the content of a file before recent changes — see what it looked like at a previous commit or point in time.
example:
mindmap_file_before { file: 'src/services/auth.ts', ref: 'HEAD~1' }
→ (returns file content from previous commit)

name: mindmap_teach
description: Teach the system a new pattern or rule specific to this project. Persists across sessions and helps future agents follow project conventions.

params.name: rule
params.description: The pattern or rule to teach

name: mindmap_get_learned
description: View all learned patterns and rules for this project. See what conventions and patterns the system has been taught.
example:
mindmap_get_learned
→ #7: All service methods must log before returning (scope: service)
→ #5: Use zod for request validation in controllers (scope: controller)
→ #3: Prefix private methods with underscore (scope: global)

name: mindmap_forget
description: Remove a previously taught rule or pattern. Use when a convention changes or a rule is no longer applicable.

params.name: ruleId
params.description: ID of the rule to remove

example:
mindmap_forget { ruleId: 3 }
→ Rule #3 removed (was: 'Prefix private methods with underscore')
